steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage reveals that small white herons prefer extensive canopy cover, while glossy ibises do not share this preference. This difference directly determines the relative effectiveness of canopy-increasing strategies for each species.

steps.title: Determine what the strategies are less likely to do
steps.content: Choice A correctly infers that canopy-increasing strategies will be less likely to attract glossy ibises than small white herons. Choices B, C, and D either introduce factors the passage doesn't address or contradict its findings. That matches choice A — A is correct.
